documents.title: Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: RPI has a mature AI infrastructure profile: a formal generative AI policy, AiMOS supercomputer, IBM-linked AI research collaboration history, and the Lally AI Academy.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute's public AI policy posture is comprehensive and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.name: Center for Computational Innovations
bodies.scope: RPI has a mature AI infrastructure profile: a formal generative AI policy, AiMOS supercomputer, IBM-linked AI research collaboration history, and the Lally AI Academy.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

initiatives.description: Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute has public AI guidance, governance, or strategy resources that frame responsible adoption, privacy, academic integrity, and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete R1 signal of AI moving from isolated experimentation toward governed, mission-linked adoption.

initiatives.title: AiMOS AI supercomputer capacity
initiatives.description: AiMOS is RPI’s eight-petaflop IBM POWER9 supercomputer for AI applications, and public CCI documentation supports a 1,576-GPU capacity count.
initiatives.significance: This closes compute capacity with one of the largest named university AI compute assets in the sweep.

investment.description: No public total acquisition or operating cost was disclosed in the cited CCI pages.

collaborations.description: AiMOS is described as an essential component of collaboration among IBM, Empire State Development, and NY CREATES.

initiatives.title: RPI and Albany Medical College AI breast cancer grant
initiatives.description: RPI and Albany Medical College researchers received a $3.3 million NCI grant to apply AI to HER2-positive breast cancer treatment.
initiatives.significance: This adds a disclosed AI research funding and partnership signal.

ai_academic_programs.name: Lally AI Academy 30-day AI product sprint

computing_infrastructure.summary: RPI’s AiMOS is an eight-petaflop IBM POWER9 supercomputer for AI applications. CCI documentation lists 16 nodes with 4 NVIDIA V100 GPUs and 252 nodes with 6 NVIDIA V100 GPUs, for 1,576 GPUs.

investments_and_gifts.title: NCI AI breast cancer treatment grant
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Use artificial intelligence to improve targeted drug therapy in HER2-positive breast cancer treatment with Albany Medical College.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for NY. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: RPI has a mature AI infrastructure profile: a formal generative AI policy, AiMOS supercomputer, IBM-linked AI research collaboration history, and the Lally AI Academy. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
No total institution-wide AI investment amount was found.
Exact enterprise AI user counts or contract values were usually not public.
RPI now has public AiMOS GPU count and AI grant evidence, but Worker 3 did not find public campus-wide LLM/Copilot seat counts or student subgroup outcome metrics.
RPI has strong research compute evidence but no public contract values for any general-purpose enterprise AI platform surfaced in targeted searches.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: research compute powerhouse.
